questions.text: The reaction rate in forward direction decreases with the passage of time because:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: According to the law of mass action, the rate of a chemical reaction is directly proportional to the product of the concentrations of the reactants. As the reaction progresses, the concentration of reactants decreases, leading to a decrease in the reaction rate in the forward direction.
The order of a reaction is a constant value that does not change during the course of the reaction. It is determined experimentally and is independent of the concentration of reactants or products.

The concentration of the product typically increases as the reaction proceeds, assuming the reaction is occurring in the forward direction. This does not account for the decrease in the forward reaction rate.

A change in temperature can affect the rate of reaction, but the question specifically asks about the decrease in the forward reaction rate over time, which is primarily due to the decrease in reactant concentrations, not temperature changes.

questions.explanation: According to law of mass action, rate of reaction is directly proportional to product of active masses of reactant. During reaction, active masses or concentration of reactants decreases. That is why, rate in forward direction decreases with decrease in concentration of reactants.

questions.text: If the energy of activation of a chemical reaction is very low, the rate of that chemical reaction is observed to be very high because:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Concentration of the reactants becomes irrelevant
options.explanation: Concentration of reactants does not become irrelevant as more reactan concentration more energy is needed.

options.text: Number of efficient or fruit collisions increase
options.explanation: Rate of reaction is directly proportional to no. of effective collision and inversely proportional to activation energy. If activation energy is very low then no. of effective collision would increase and reaction would be very high.

options.text: Reaction proceeds without any transition state
options.explanation: Every reaction proceeds with transition state.

options.text: Molecules of the reactants move slowly
options.explanation: As rate of reaction increases, reactant molecules move faster.

questions.explanation: Rate of reaction is directly proportional to no. of effective collision and inversely proportional to activation energy. If activation energy is very low then no. of effective collision would increase and reaction would be very high.

questions.text: Role of a catalyst in a chemical reaction is to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A positive catalyst increase rate of reaction, decreases activation energy, decrease equilibrium time and changes mechanism of reaction A catalyst is a chemical substance which helps to increase the rate of the reaction. - It makes the reaction proceed faster by adjusting the activation energy of that reaction or by changing the mechanism of the reaction. - It does not get consumed by itself in the reaction.

It increases the rate of reaction by lowering activation energy. A catalyst is a chemical substance which helps to increase the rate of the reaction. - It makes the reaction proceed faster by adjusting the activation energy of that reaction or by changing the mechanism of the reaction. - It does not get consumed by itself in the reaction.
It only increases rate of reaction.

It only increases rate of reaction. A catalyst is a chemical substance which helps to increase the rate of the reaction. - It makes the reaction proceed faster by adjusting the activation energy of that reaction or by changing the mechanism of the reaction. - It does not get consumed by itself in the reaction.

questions.explanation: A positive catalyst increase rate of reaction, decreases activation energy, decrease equilibrium time and changes mechanism of reaction.

questions.text: The rate constant k is 0.693min-1. The half life for the first order reaction will be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. You can calculate the half-life using the formula:t½ = (0.693 / k)Substituting the given rate constant (k = 0.693 min⁻¹) into the equation yields:t½ = (0.693 / 0.693 min⁻¹) = 1 minute.

This option is incorrect. While it reflects the value of the rate constant, it does not represent the half-life calculated using the correct formula for first-order reactions, which requires dividing 0.693 by the rate constant.
This option is also incorrect. A half-life of 2 minutes would imply a different value for the rate constant, which does not align with the given k value of 0.693 min⁻¹.
This option is incorrect as well. A half-life of 4 minutes is not possible with the provided rate constant of 0.693 min⁻¹, which will yield a much shorter half-life.

questions.explanation: It can be calculated using the following formula: t½ = (0.693 / k) Given that the rate constant (k) is 0.693 min⁻¹, you can calculate the half-life as follows: t½ = (0.693 / 0.693 min⁻¹) = 1 minute.

questions.text: The increase in reaction rate as a result of increase in temperature from 10K to 100K is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Temperature increases from 10K to 100K is a 90K increase, corresponding to 9 intervals of 10K. The reaction rate approximately doubles with each 10K increase: Rate increase = (2)9 = 512.
This option incorrectly calculates the rate increase. The actual calculation should use powers of 2 for each 10K interval.
This option underestimates the rate increase. Correct calculation using 9 intervals of 10K should yield a result of 512, not 400.
This option significantly underestimates the rate increase, demonstrating a misunderstanding of the exponential nature of the rate change with temperature.

questions.explanation: Rate roughly doubles every 10K. Temperature rises from 10K → 100K → 9 steps of 10K. So, rate increase = 2 =512.

questions.text: Which method for rate determination is useful for those reaction, which involve small volume change in solutions?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Refractometry is the method of measuring substances refractive index and assess their composition or purity. Refractometry is a technique that measures how light is refracted when it passes through a given substance.

A dilatometer is a scientific instrument that measures volume changes caused by a physical or chemical process. A familiar application of a dilatometer is the mercury-in-glass thermometer, in which the change in volume of the liquid column is read from a graduated scale.

The optical rotation is the angle through which the plane of polarization is rotated when polarized light passes through a layer of a liquid. Optical rotation is the effect which is determined by the concentration of chiral molecules and their molecular structure in a substance.
The amount of light absorbed is directly proportional to the concentration of the solute in the solution and thickness of the solution under analysis.

questions.explanation: Dilatometire method is applicable for the measurement of rate of those reactions which involve small volume change in the solutions.

questions.text: The 2nd order reaction becomes 1st order when:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: One of the reactants is limiting
options.explanation: If concentration of a reactant remains constant (because it is a catalyst or because it is in excess with respect to other reactants), its concentration can be included in rate constant thus making(causing) the reaction to be pseudo first order or sometimes pseudo second order reaction.

options.text: One of the reactants is in large excess
options.explanation: If concentration of a reactant remains constant (because it is a catalyst or because it is in excess with respect to other reactants), its concentration can be included in rate constant thus making(causing) the reaction to be pseudo first order or sometimes pseudo second order reaction.

options.text: None of the reactants is in large excess
options.explanation: If B is in excess, it becomes pseudo second order. It is generally accepted that third order reactions are hard to occur.

The substance taken in small amount controls the rate and the order is noted with respect to that substance. If the cone. Of any reactant is taken in excess it will not involve in rate determination step.

questions.explanation: The substance taken in small amount controls the rate and the order is noted with respect to that substance. If the cone. Of any reactant is taken in excess it will not involve in rate determination step.

questions.text: Hydrolysis of ethyl acetate into acetic acid and ethanol in the presence of mineral acid is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The order is a non-integer that indicates chemical chain reaction or other complex reaction mechanism. The order of a chain reaction can be rationalized using the steady state approximation for the concentration of reactive intermediates such as free radicals.
In hydrolysis of ester water is in large excess therefore it is pseudo 1st order reaction.

A first-order reaction is one in which the rate of reaction is proportional to the concentration of the reactant. To put it another way, doubling the concentration doubles the reaction rate. A first-order reaction can have one or two reactants, as in the case of the decomposition reaction.
Second order reactions can be defined as chemical reactions wherein the sum of the exponents in the corresponding rate law of the chemical reaction is equal to two.

questions.explanation: Although the reaction is second order overall (first order in ethyl acetate and first order in H₂O), water is in large excess, so its concentration is essentially constant.This makes the reaction appear to follow first-order kinetics, called a pseudo first-order reaction.

questions.text: The rate law of a reaction is, rate = k[A]2[B]. On doubling the concentration of both A and B, the rate of reaction increases_time.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Based on the rate law, doubling [A] and [B] would result in a rate increase of 8 times, not 4. The rate is proportional to [A]^2[B], so doubling [A] results in a 4-fold increase and doubling [B] results in a 2-fold increase, leading to an 8-fold increase overall.

The rate is not affected by doubling [A] and [B] in such a way to produce a 9-fold increase. The rate law is rate = k[A]^2[B], so doubling [A] results in a 4-fold increase and doubling [B] results in a 2-fold increase, leading to an 8-fold increase overall.

This is correct. The rate law is rate = k[A]^2[B]. Doubling [A] results in a 4-fold increase (since [A] is squared), and doubling [B] results in a 2-fold increase. Thus, the overall rate increase is 4 x 2 = 8 times.

Doubling [A] and [B] does not lead to a 2-fold increase. According to the rate law rate = k[A]^2[B], doubling [A] leads to a 4-fold increase and doubling [B] leads to a 2-fold increase, resulting in an 8-fold increase overall.

questions.explanation: The rate law of a reaction is given by:rate = k[A]2 [B].If we double the concentration of both A and B, the new rate of reaction will be:new rate = k([2A])2([2B]) = k(4[A] )(2[B]) = 8k[A] [B].Therefore, the new rate of reaction is 8 times the original rate of reaction.

questions.text: The half-life period of zero order reaction is equal to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This formula is applicable for first-order reactions, where the half-life is independent of the initial concentration.

This is the correct formula for a zero order reaction, where the half-life is directly proportional to the initial concentration of the reactant (a) and inversely proportional to the rate constant (K).
This formula does not correspond to any common order reaction. It is incorrect for zero order reactions.
This formula is incorrect and does not represent the half-life of zero, first, or second order reactions.

questions.explanation: For a zero order reaction, the half-life is given by the formula t1/2 = a/2K, where a is the initial concentration of the reactant and K is the rate constant. This means that the half-life is directly proportional to the initial concentration of the reactant. Option B is correct. Option A is the formula for the half-life of a first-order reaction, which is not applicable here. Option C and D provide incorrect formulas that do not apply to zero order reactions.

questions.text: For which of the reaction, half-life is inversely proportional to the initial concentration of the reactants?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: For a zero-order reaction, the half-life is given by t1/2 = [A]0/2k, making it directly proportional to the initial concentration of the reactants.
The half-life of a first-order reaction is t1/2 = 0.693/k, which is independent of the initial concentration.
For a second-order reaction, the half-life is t1/2 = 1/(k[A]0), meaning it is inversely proportional to the initial concentration of the reactants.
While more complex, the half-life for third-order reactions is not inversely proportional to the initial concentration in a straightforward manner like second-order reactions.

questions.explanation: In a second-order reaction, the half-life formula t1/2 = 1/(k[A]0) shows that the half-life is inversely proportional to the initial concentration of the reactants. This means that as the initial concentration increases, the half-life decreases. For a zero-order reaction, the half-life is directly proportional to the initial concentration. In a first-order reaction, the half-life is independent of initial concentration, while third-order reactions have a more complex relationship that is not simply inversely proportional.

questions.text: Oxidation-reduction reactions are _ than ionic reactions.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Redox reactions are slower than ionic reactions because they involve the transfer of electrons, which is a slower process than the movement of ions in solution.
Redox reactions are slower than ionic reactions because they involve the transfer of electrons, which is a slower process than the movement of ions in solution.
Redox reactions are slower than ionic reactions because they involve the transfer of electrons, which is a slower process than the movement of ions in solution.
This statement suggests that from the above-mentioned options none is correct, however, option C is correct.

questions.explanation: Redox reactions are slower than ionic reactions because they involve the transfer of electrons, which is a slower process than the movement of ions in solution. Additionally, redox reactions often involve the formation of intermediates, which can slow the overall reaction. Finally, redox reactions often require catalysts to occur at a reasonable rate, while ionic reactions do not.

questions.text: The concentration of gas can be increased by increasing _ of the gas.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Increasing temperature raises the kinetic energy of gas molecules, leading to more frequent collisions, but it does not change the concentration of the gas.
Increasing the volume of a gas decreases its concentration because the same number of molecules are spread over a larger space.
Increasing pressure compresses the gas molecules into a smaller volume, thereby increasing the concentration of the gas.
Specific gravity is a measure of density relative to water and does not affect gas concentration.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct because increasing the pressure on a gas compresses its molecules, thereby increasing its concentration. This is consistent with the ideal gas law, which relates pressure and volume inversely, such that when pressure goes up (at constant temperature), volume goes down, leading to higher concentration. Option A is incorrect because temperature affects kinetic energy, not concentration. Option B is incorrect because increasing the volume of a gas decreases its concentration. Option D is incorrect because specific gravity is unrelated to the concentration of a gas.

questions.text: The slope of concentration time graph gives value of
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The y-axis already represents concentration, and the slope reflects how this concentration changes over time, not the concentration itself.
The concentration-time graph does not typically include the catalyst amount as it remains unchanged during the reaction and is not a reactant or product.
The y-axis of the graph indicates concentration, but the slope gives the rate of change of concentration, not the concentration of products directly.
The slope of a concentration-time graph indicates the rate of reaction, which is the change in concentration of a reactant or product per unit time.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D: Rate of reaction. The slope of a concentration-time graph for a chemical reaction directly reflects the rate at which the concentration of reactants or products changes with time, which is defined as the reaction rate.Options A and C are incorrect because the y-axis already represents concentration, and the slope indicates the rate of change, not the concentration itself. Option B is incorrect because the amount of catalyst is not typically represented in a concentration-time graph, as it does not change throughout the reaction.

questions.text: The optical rotation method is concerned with _ of reacting substances.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The refractive index, also known as the index of refraction, is a fundamental property of a material that describes how light propagates through it. It is a dimensionless quantity that indicates how much light is bent or refracted as it passes from one medium into another. Refratrometric method is concerned with refractive index.

The optical rotation method is concerned with the study of the chiral or optically active properties of reacting substances. Chiral molecules are asymmetric and exist in two distinct forms known as enantiomers. These enantiomers rotate the plane of polarized light in opposite directions, either clockwise (dextrorotatory) or counterclockwise (levorotatory).
Concentration refers to the amount of solute present in a given amount of solvent or solution.
Density is a physical property that measures the amount of mass contained in a given volume of a substance.

questions.explanation: The optical rotation method is concerned with the study of the chiral or optically active properties of reacting substances, therefore, option B is correct.

questions.text: The rate at any one instant during the intervals is called as
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The average rate of a chemical reaction is the change in the concentration of a reactant or product over a specified time interval. It represents the overall rate of the reaction during that interval.

The rate of a chemical reaction at any one instant during the reaction is called the "instantaneous rate." The instantaneous rate refers to the rate of change of reactant concentrations or product concentrations at a specific moment in time. It represents the rate of reaction at an infinitesimally small time interval, often at a particular time t. The instantaneous rate can vary during the course of the reaction as the concentrations of reactants and products change.

The reaction rate is a measure of how quickly a chemical reaction takes place. The reaction rate is an essential parameter in studying the kinetics of a reaction, which deals with the study of reaction rates and the factors that influence them. The reaction rate is typically expressed as the rate of change of the concentration of a reactant or product with respect to time.
This statement states that the above-mentioned options are wrong, however, that's not correct as option B is correct.

questions.explanation: The rate of a chemical reaction at any one instant during the reaction is called the "instantaneous rate", therefore option B is correct.

questions.text: What is the effect of decrease in reactants concentration on protium concentration?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Decreases products is not correct because the decrease in protium concentration will cause the reaction to slow down, and the amount of products produced will be lower. However, the decrease in protium concentration will also cause the equilibrium constant to shift to the right, which will cause more products to be produced.

The effect of a decrease in reactants concentration on protium concentration increases the product concentration. The reason for this is that the concentration of protium is directly proportional to the concentration of the reactants. If the concentration of the reactants decreases, the concentration of protium will also decrease. This will cause the reaction to slow down, and the amount of products produced will be lower.
Affects negligibly is not correct because the decrease in protium concentration will have a significant effect on the reaction rate and the product concentration.

Improves reaction efficiency is not correct because the decrease in protium concentration will actually decrease the reaction efficiency. This is because the reaction will be slower, and the amount of products produced will be lower.

questions.explanation: The effect of a decrease in reactants concentration on protium concentration increases the product concentration. The reason for this is that the concentration of protium is directly proportional to the concentration of the reactants. If the concentration of the reactants decreases, the concentration of protium will also decrease. This will cause the reaction to slow down, and the amount of products produced will be lower.

questions.text: If the energy of the activated complex lies close to energy of reactants, it means that reaction is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Slow is not correct because if the energy of the activated complex is close to the energy of the reactants, it means that the reaction does not require a lot of energy to get over the activation barrier. This means that the reaction will be fast.

Endothermic is not correct because the energy of the activated complex is not necessarily related to the enthalpy change of the reaction. The enthalpy change of the reaction is the difference in energy between the reactants and the products. In an exothermic reaction, the products have a lower energy than the reactants. In an endothermic reaction, the products have a higher energy than the reactants.

Exothermic is not correct because the energy of the activated complex is not necessarily related to the rate of the reaction. The rate of the reaction is determined by the activation energy and the collision frequency. If the activation energy is low, the reaction will be fast, regardless of whether the reaction is exothermic or endothermic.

The activated complex is the highest-energy state of a reaction. If the energy of the activated complex is close to the energy of the reactants, it means that the reaction does not require a lot of energy to get over the activation barrier. This means that the reaction will be Exothermic and fast.

questions.explanation: The activated complex is the highest-energy state of a reaction. If the energy of the activated complex is close to the energy of the reactants, it means that the reaction does not require a lot of energy to get over the activation barrier. This means that the reaction will be Exothermic and fast.

questions.text: If Ef and Er are activation energies of forward and reverse reaction and the reaction is known to be exothermic then,
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ef > Er is incorrect because in an exothermic reaction, the products have lower energy than the reactants. Thus, the activation energy for the forward reaction (Ef) should be less than that for the reverse reaction (Er).

This is correct. In an exothermic reaction, the energy of the products is lower than that of the reactants, which means the activation energy for the forward reaction (Ef) is less than that for the reverse reaction (Er).

Ef >>> Er is incorrect because it implies that the activation energy for the forward reaction is far greater than for the reverse, which contradicts the nature of an exothermic reaction. In such reactions, Ef is actually less than Er.

Ef = Er is incorrect because if the activation energies for both forward and reverse reactions were equal, it would imply a thermally neutral process, not an exothermic one. In exothermic reactions, Ef is less than Er.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Ef < Er. In an exothermic reaction, energy is released, which means the products have a lower energy level than the reactants. Consequently, the activation energy required to proceed from reactants to products (Ef) is lower than the activation energy required to go from products back to reactants (Er).Option A, Ef > Er, is incorrect because it suggests the forward reaction needs more energy than the reverse, which is not the case for exothermic reactions. Option C, Ef >>> Er, is incorrect for the same reason and suggests an unrealistic energy discrepancy. Option D, Ef = Er, is incorrect because it implies no net energy change, contradicting the exothermic nature where Ef must be less than Er.

questions.text: For a single step reaction A ->B + 2C, the rate law is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because rate laws or rate equations are mathematical expressions that describe the relationship between the rate of a chemical reaction and the concentration of its reactants.
This option is incorrect because B is on the product side and we do not incorporate products while stating the rate law.
This is the correct option as here we have taken only the reactant i.e. A to form the rate law equation.
This option is incorrect because while stating the rate law equation, only reactants are considered, whereas here B is present on the products side.

questions.explanation: Rate laws or rate equations are mathematical expressions that describe the relationship between the rate of a chemical reaction and the concentration of its reactants. Rate = k[A]x[B]y Where A and B are the reactants raised to power x and y which are their stoichiometric coefficients in the balanced equation and k is the proportionality constant of the reaction.In this particular reaction, only A is on the reactant side and has no power so the rate equation becomes. rate=k[A]. The rest of the options also include products in the rate equation (options B and D) and option A does not include reactant A in the rate equation, hence these are incorrect.
